channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump denies conflict of interest over crypto. And, Vatican excommunicates rebel group
item.description: Trump and his family earned over $1 billion last year through cryptocurrency ventures and other businesses. And, the Vatican declared that the traditionalist Society of St. Pius X has entered schism.
item.content:encoded:
Trump and his family earned over $1 billion last year through cryptocurrency ventures and other businesses. And, the Vatican declared that the traditionalist Society of St. Pius X has entered schism.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Going outside in this heat? Follow these guidelines to stay safe
item.description: Man, it's a hot one! Don't go out in this summer's heat wave before you arm yourself with these tips and a really big water bottle.
item.content:encoded:
Man, it's a hot one! Don't go out in this summer's heat wave before you arm yourself with these tips and a really big water bottle.

(Image credit: SelÃ§uk Acar/Anadolu)
item.dc:creator: Carmel Wroth

item.title: Federal civil rights data holds schools accountable. Under Trump, it's 6 months late
item.description: The Education Department has long collected civil rights data about things like bullying, harassment and disability services in schools, but it hasn't made the latest information public.
item.content:encoded: The Education Department has long collected civil rights data about things like bullying, harassment and disability services in schools, but it hasn't made the latest information public.
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: The U.S. healthcare system is in crisis. A Supreme Court ruling could make things worse
item.description: Mass deportations would be felt across hospitals and emergency rooms, which already face persistent staffing shortfalls. The long-term healthcare sector will suffer the greatest disruptions, experts say.
item.content:encoded:
Mass deportations would be felt across hospitals and emergency rooms, which already face persistent staffing shortfalls. The long-term healthcare sector will suffer the greatest disruptions, experts say.

(Image credit: Spencer Platt)
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Romo

item.title: Vatican declares Society of St. Pius X in schism, excommunicates bishops
item.description: The Vatican responded Thursday to a traditionalist society that consecrated bishops without the pope's consent, declaring the Society of St. Pius X in schism and excommunicating its bishops and priests.
item.content:encoded:
The Vatican responded Thursday to a traditionalist society that consecrated bishops without the pope's consent, declaring the Society of St. Pius X in schism and excommunicating its bishops and priests.

(Image credit: Baz Ratner)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. job market slows in June
item.description: Employers added 57,000 jobs in June, the Labor Department said on Friday, as jobs growth slowed from the previous two months, while the unemployment rate ticked down to 4.2%.
item.content:encoded: Employers added 57,000 jobs in June, the Labor Department said on Friday, as jobs growth slowed from the previous two months, while the unemployment rate ticked down to 4.2%.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Democratic socialists pose a challenge for the party as midterms approach
item.description: A string of high-profile victories by democratic socialists is posing a challenge for Democrats as they look ahead to midterms and seek a path back to the majority in Congress.
item.content:encoded: A string of high-profile victories by democratic socialists is posing a challenge for Democrats as they look ahead to midterms and seek a path back to the majority in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: The rise of democratic socialists and what it means for the party
item.description: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Brad Lander, Democratic nominee for New York's 10th Congressional District, about the rise of democratic socialists in the Democratic Party.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Brad Lander, Democratic nominee for New York's 10th Congressional District, about the rise of democratic socialists in the Democratic Party.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Russia hits Ukraine's capital with a massive drone and missile attack, killing at least 18
item.description: Russia launched a large-scale attack on Ukraine's capital overnight into Thursday, with ballistic and cruise missiles and drones, killing several people.
item.content:encoded:
Russia launched a large-scale attack on Ukraine's capital overnight into Thursday, with ballistic and cruise missiles and drones, killing several people.

(Image credit: Danylo Antoniuk)
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: U.S. and Iran hold separate meetings in Qatar and agree to continue discussions
item.description: U.S. and Iranian negotiators met separately on Wednesday with Qatari and Pakistani mediators, with "positive progress made," and they agreed to continue discussions, host Qatar said.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. and Iranian negotiators met separately on Wednesday with Qatari and Pakistani mediators, with "positive progress made," and they agreed to continue discussions, host Qatar said.

(Image credit: Amirhosein Khorgooi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
